title: Coordinate systems
code: df_path_gal <- data.frame(   l = c(110, 110),   b = c(-4, 60),   g = 1 )  ggplot(df_path_gal, aes(l, b, group = g)) +   geom_path(colour = "blue", linewidth = 1) +   coord_galactic() +   theme_light() df_path_eq <- data.frame(   ra = c(0, 60),   dec = c(30, 30),   g = 1 )  ggplot(df_path_eq, aes(ra, dec, group = g)) +   geom_path() +   coord_equatorial() +   scale_eq_ra(breaks = seq(0, 330, by = 30)) +   theme_light() # 5) Equatorial + geom_segment df_seg_eq <- data.frame(   x = 30, y = -10,   xend = 120, yend = 40 )  ggplot(df_seg_eq) +   geom_segment(     aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend),     linewidth = 1, colour = "orange",     arrow = arrow(type = "closed", length = unit(0.1, "inches"))   ) +   coord_equatorial() +   theme_light()

what: Plot sky equator on galactic plane
title: Coordinate systems
code: library(dplyr) library(latex2exp) # devtools::install_github("uskovgs/xrayr")   df1 <- data.frame(   l = c(120, 120, 0, 0, 0, 120),   b = c(0, 60, 60, 60, 0, 0) )  coords_eq0 <- xrayr::ra_dec(0:359, rep(0.000001, 360)) coords_eq0_gal <- xrayr::to_galactic(coords_eq0) df_eq0 <- data.frame(   l = coords_eq0_gal$l,   b = coords_eq0_gal$b,   ra = xrayr::ra(coords_eq0) )  ggplot(df1, aes(l, b)) +   geom_path(colour = "blue", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_path(     data = df_eq0, aes(l, b), colour = "red",     linetype = "dotted"   ) +   geom_text(     data = dplyr::filter(df_eq0, ra %% 30 == 0),     aes(label = latex2exp::TeX(paste0("$", ra, "^\\circ$"), output = "character")),     parse = TRUE,     vjust = -0.5,     size = 3,     color = "red"   ) +   coord_galactic()

title: Authors and Citation
text: Uskov G (2026). ggsky: Galactic Equatorial Coordinate Implementation 'ggplot2'. R package version 0.1.0, https://uskovgs.github.io/ggsky/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {ggsky: Galactic and Equatorial Coordinate Implementation for 'ggplot2'},   author = {Grigory Uskov},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://uskovgs.github.io/ggsky/}, }

what: Galactic and Equatorial Coordinate Implementation for ggplot2
title: Galactic and Equatorial Coordinate Implementation for ggplot2
text: ggsky extension ggplot2 draw sky maps : galactic coordinates (coord_galactic()) equatorial coordinates (coord_equatorial()) includes custom coordinate systems helper scales longitude/latitude labels.

title: Galactic and Equatorial Coordinate Implementation for ggplot2
Galactic and Equatorial Coordinate Implementation for ggplot2
code: library(ggplot2)   library(ggsky)    N <- 100   df1 <- data.frame(     x = runif(N, 0, 360),     y = runif(N, -90, 90)   )    ggplot(df1, aes(x, y)) +     geom_point() +     coord_galactic() +     labs(title = "Galactic coordinate system")    ggplot(df1, aes(x, y)) +     geom_point() +     labs(title = "Equatorial coordinate system") +     coord_equatorial()

title: Equatorial Hammer Coordinate System — coord_equatorial
text: Coordinate system sky maps equatorial coordinates using Hammer-Aitoff projection.

title: Equatorial Hammer Coordinate System — coord_equatorial
code: coord_equatorial(   clip = "on",   label_offset_lon = 0.025,   label_offset_lat = 0.035,   munch_deg = 1,   pad_top_pt = NULL,   pad_bottom_pt = NULL,   pad_left_pt = NULL,   pad_right_pt = NULL,   clip_on_boundaries = TRUE )

title: Equatorial Hammer Coordinate System — coord_equatorial
text: clip Character scalar. Passed `ggplot2` coordinate clipping (`""` `""`). label_offset_lon Numeric scalar npc units. Vertical offset right-ascension labels relative equator. label_offset_lat Numeric scalar npc units. Outward offset declination labels relative projection outline. munch_deg Numeric scalar. Maximum angular step (degrees) used segment paths polygon edges along great circles projection. pad_top_pt, pad_bottom_pt, pad_left_pt, pad_right_pt Optional numeric scalars (points) used reserve external space axis text. `NULL` enables automatic sizing. clip_on_boundaries Logical scalar. `TRUE`, draws outside mask geoms visually clipped projection boundary.

title: Equatorial Hammer Coordinate System — coord_equatorial
text: `ggplot2` coordinate object (`ggproto` instance inheriting   `CoordEquatorial`) added plot.

title: Equatorial Hammer Coordinate System — coord_equatorial
code: library(ggplot2)  df <- data.frame(   ra = c(0, 30, 60, 90, 120),   dec = c(-20, -5, 10, 25, 15) )  ggplot(df, aes(ra, dec)) +   geom_path() +   coord_equatorial() +   scale_eq_ra(breaks = seq(0, 330, by = 30)) +   scale_eq_dec(breaks = seq(-60, 60, by = 30))

title: Galactic Hammer Coordinate System — coord_galactic
text: Coordinate system sky maps galactic coordinates using Hammer-Aitoff projection.

title: Galactic Hammer Coordinate System — coord_galactic
code: coord_galactic(   clip = "on",   label_offset_lon = 0.025,   label_offset_lat = 0.035,   munch_deg = 1,   pad_top_pt = NULL,   pad_bottom_pt = NULL,   pad_left_pt = NULL,   pad_right_pt = NULL,   clip_on_boundaries = TRUE )

title: Galactic Hammer Coordinate System — coord_galactic
text: clip Character scalar. Passed `ggplot2` coordinate clipping (`""` `""`). label_offset_lon Numeric scalar npc units. Vertical offset galactic-longitude labels relative equator. label_offset_lat Numeric scalar npc units. Outward offset galactic-latitude labels relative projection outline. munch_deg Numeric scalar. Maximum angular step (degrees) used segment paths polygon edges along great circles projection. pad_top_pt, pad_bottom_pt, pad_left_pt, pad_right_pt Optional numeric scalars (points) used reserve external space axis text. `NULL` enables automatic sizing. clip_on_boundaries Logical scalar. `TRUE`, draws outside mask geoms visually clipped projection boundary.

title: Galactic Hammer Coordinate System — coord_galactic
text: `ggplot2` coordinate object (`ggproto` instance inheriting   `CoordGalactic`) added plot.

title: Galactic Hammer Coordinate System — coord_galactic
code: library(ggplot2)  df <- data.frame(   lon = c(0, 30, 60, 90, 120),   lat = c(-20, -5, 10, 25, 15) )  ggplot(df, aes(lon, lat)) +   geom_path() +   coord_galactic() +   scale_gal_lon(breaks = seq(0, 330, by = 30)) +   scale_gal_lat(breaks = seq(-60, 60, by = 30))
